body_txt: A palindrome is a word or longer sequence that remains the same when read backwards. Just as writers like to play with anagrams or acronyms/initialisms , so indeed do they seize opportunities to use palindromes. However, since it's hard to come up with a palindrome—let alone a meaningful one—it's unlikely to be a plot point, and is usually a result of the Rule of Fun . Not to be confused with Sdrawkcab Name , though the two can overlap a little. Has nothing to do with Sarah Palin . note "Palindrome" comes from Greek πάλιν "again" and δρóμος "way, direction", while "Palin" the surname is British in origin, so likely unrelated.
